Title: Ancestral sequence reconstruction of the CYP711 family reveals functional divergence in strigolactone biosynthetic enzymes associated with gene duplication events in monocot grasses

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Name: CYP711A ancestral enzymes (N0, N10, N25, N64, N71, N153, N193, N271)
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Ancestral land plant/angiosperm/monocot/eudicot lineages reconstructed from multiple plant species
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli in vitro membrane assay system
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: DH5α qF'IQ / BL21-derived strains used for expression backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 family CYP711A; heme-thiolate monooxygenase; conserved P450 motifs including EXXR and FXXGXRXCXG
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Conversion of carlactone to downstream strigolactone intermediates and diversification/hydroxylation of canonical strigolactone-like scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor pool and directly diversifies canonical strigolactone scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Carlactone (CL); GR24 and purified GR24 stereoisomers as surrogate substrates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Carlactonoic acid (CLA); multiple hydroxylated GR24 +16 Da products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes carlactone to carlactonoic acid and catalyzes regio-/stereospecific hydroxylation of GR24 surrogate strigolactone scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant hormone controlling development and rhizosphere signaling, including mycorrhizal interactions and parasitic weed germination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CYP711A ancestors catalyze conserved CL to CLA activity and diversified hydroxylations on SL-like scaffolds, supporting a causal role in strigolactone structural diversification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ancestral sequence reconstruction; heterologous expression in E. coli with ATR1; membrane enzyme assays; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling using CL and GR24 isomers
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Microsomal membrane/endoplasmic reticulum-associated P450 (inferred from family characteristics and membrane-anchor motifs)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous bacterial expression in membrane fractions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro NADPH-dependent assay conditions; use of GR24 stereoisomers as substrate surrogates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple resurrected ancestral CYP711As converted CL to CLA, indicating conserved ancestral MAX1-like activity. 2. None of the tested monocot ancestors N25 or N71 showed A2-type conversion to 4-deoxyorobanchol in the CL assay. 3. All tested ancestors hydroxylated GR24 to multiple products, revealing inherent catalytic promiscuity. 4. These data support an ancestral role of CYP711A enzymes in strigolactone diversification before angiosperm radiation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Native plant-level growth or yield tradeoffs were not tested; broad substrate promiscuity may cause off-target product formation in engineering contexts
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Sequence mining from NCBI, UniProt and 1KP transcriptome resources; no accession series for omics deposition reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Ancestral CYP711As provide candidate biocatalysts for producing diverse native and new-to-nature strigolactone analogues for agrochemical and metabolic engineering applications
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This entry summarizes the experimentally tested resurrected CYP711A ancestors rather than a single natural gene; structural identification of hydroxylated GR24 products remains incomplete without NMR

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli in vitro membrane assay system
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: DH5α qF'IQ / pCW membrane expression system
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Canonical strigolactone diversification, especially strigol-type-like scaffold hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Carlactonoic acid; multiple GR24 +16 Da hydroxylation products, especially RT 3.35 products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts CL to CLA and stereoselectively hydroxylates GR24-like strigolactone scaffolds with strong preference for the (+)-GR24 isomer
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates plant architecture and rhizosphere interactions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: N71 showed increased catalytic activity toward GR24 and high stereoselectivity toward the strigol-type-mimicking (+)-GR24 isomer, implying an ancestral role in strigol-type SL diversification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Equal-P450 membrane assays; LC-MS/MS product quantification; purified GR24 stereoisomer assays
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key functional divergence point within monocot grass CYP711A evolution; not proven rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in E. coli membranes, though lower than some other ancestors
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro substrate stereoisomer comparisons with purified GR24 isomers
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. N71 converted CL to CLA but not detectable 4DO in the assay used. 2. N71 produced around threefold more RT 3.35 hydroxylated GR24 product than the next most active ancestor under normalized P450 conditions. 3. N71 selectively accepted the 5DS-like (+)-GR24 isomer and not the orobanchol-like or inactive stereoisomers. 4. N71 is the clearest evidence for functional divergence following monocot grass CYP711A duplication.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential catalytic promiscuity could complicate precise engineering of single desired SL products
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising biocatalyst for selective synthesis or engineering of strigol-type strigolactone derivatives and for probing how gene duplication generated functional novelty
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate sequence determinants discussed include V377I, L489V, E482Q, E484Q, and L222Q, but these were not mutationally validated in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli in vitro membrane assay system
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and diversifies SL scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: GR24; native CL-related function discussed contextually
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: GR24 +16 Da hydroxylation products in assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates GR24 surrogate substrate; known extant A2-type CYP711A from rice included as comparison
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant developmental and rhizosphere signaling hormone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Os900 produced hydroxylated GR24 products, serving as an extant monocot grass comparator for ancestral CYP711A function
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous membrane assay and LC-MS/MS product detection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Very low detectable holoenzyme expression in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os900 was included as an extant rice CYP711A comparator despite poor expression. 2. It generated GR24 +16 Da products. 3. Because expression was below robust quantification, direct activity comparisons with well-expressed ancestors were limited.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful benchmark extant monocot CYP711A for future engineering of rice strigolactone profiles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although biologically important, this paper did not re-establish full native reaction sequence for Os900 in planta

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli in vitro membrane assay system
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: GR24 +16 Da hydroxylation products in assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates GR24 surrogate substrate; extant A3-type CYP711A comparator in rice
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant developmental and rhizosphere signaling hormone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Os1400 formed hydroxylated GR24 products, consistent with downstream scaffold oxidation capacity of extant rice CYP711A enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous membrane assay and LC-MS/MS product detection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Very low detectable holoenzyme expression in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os1400 served as an extant rice A3-type comparator. 2. It generated GR24 +16 Da products in the heterologous assay. 3. Direct quantitative comparison was constrained by poor expression.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low heterologous expression limited comparative interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant extant enzyme for engineering downstream SL oxidation in cereals
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper's main focus was ancestral functional divergence rather than full characterization of Os1400

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Engineered carotenogenic E. coli strain BL21-EcoFlex58 and related constructs
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: DWARF27 isomerase lacking plastid transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Iron-binding β-carotene isomerase involved in SL precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream precursor formation from carotenoids to carlactone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Isomerized carotenoid substrate for CCD7/CCD8 leading to CL
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts with CCD7 and CCD8 to convert β-carotene toward carlactone in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of the engineered three-enzyme module enabling on-demand CL production; Trx fusion improved CL titre in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial fusion-partner engineering and LC-MS/MS quantification of CL
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid-targeted in native plant; transit peptide removed for bacterial expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusion partner optimization (Trx, MBP, GlpF) in whole-cell bacterial production system
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsD27ΔTP was used with PsCCD7 and PsCCD8 to build a CL-producing E. coli strain. 2. Trx fusion to OsD27ΔTP contributed to a 20-fold increase in CL titre compared with no-fusion constructs. 3. The engineered strain reached 112 ± 22 μg l−1 CL after 48 h.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: This was a tool-building module; no plant phenotype tradeoff tested
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful synthetic biology module for scalable CL supply to study or engineer downstream SL enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive enabling gene rather than the central evolutionary focus of the paper

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Engineered carotenogenic E. coli strain BL21-EcoFlex58 and related constructs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream precursor formation from carotenoids to carlactone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Isomerized carotenoid substrate from D27 step
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Apo-carotenoid intermediate for CCD8 and CL formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves carotenoid precursor en route to carlactone in engineered bacterial system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of the engineered CL production module; Trx fusion improved CL output in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial fusion-partner engineering and LC-MS/MS quantification of CL
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusion partner optimization in bacterial production context
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsCCD7 was one of three enzymes required for CL biosynthesis in the bacterial chassis. 2. Trx fusion of PsCCD7 was part of the best-performing strain. 3. Together with OsD27ΔTP and PsCCD8, it enabled substantial CL production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway component for microbial production of strigolactone precursors
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive enabling gene rather than main evolutionary focal gene

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Engineered carotenogenic E. coli strain BL21-EcoFlex58 and related constructs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal upstream precursor formation to carlactone
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream to midstream; terminal CL-forming step in module
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor to target hormone pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts apo-carotenoid intermediate to carlactone in the engineered bacterial pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of the engineered CL production module; MBP fusion contributed to highest CL titre in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial fusion-partner engineering and LC-MS/MS quantification of CL
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusion partner optimization in bacterial production context
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsCCD8 completed the three-enzyme CL biosynthetic module in engineered E. coli. 2. An MBP fusion of PsCCD8 was present in the best-producing strain. 3. The optimized module yielded 112 ± 22 μg l−1 CL after 48 h.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for microbial CL biosynthesis platforms feeding downstream CYP711A biocatalysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive enabling gene rather than central evolutionary focus
